rulings.Case Title: TYRONE L. ADAMS VS. CHARLES L. EASLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion Requesting Direct Victim Restitution And Relief From Judgment Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint And Relief From Judgment Denying Victim Restitution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Tyrone Adams; Verified Affidavits Tyrone Adams
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 12, 2022, line 1. PLAINTIFF TYRONE ADAMS' Motion Requesting Direct Victim Restitution And Relief From Judgment Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint And Relief From Judgment Denying Victim Restitution Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Relief from Judgment Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff has put forward no factual or legal basis for this Court to conclude that res judicata did not apply to bar Plaintiff's claims. Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Relief from Judgement Denying Victim Restitution is DENIED. The Court is unclear which judgment Plaintiff is referring to. To the extent Plaintiff is referring to the criminal misdemeanor order entered in Sutter County, Plaintiff has provided no authority allowing this Court to set aside another court's order. Plaintiff's Motion Requesting Direct Victim Restitution is DENIED. Plaintiff is not entitled to restitution because all claims have either been adjudicated or dismissed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN MUNETON VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writs Of Administrative Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 And Ordinary Mandamus Ccp 1085
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 12, 2022, line 2. PETITIONER MARTIN MUNETON's Motion For Writs Of Administrative Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 And Ordinary Mandamus Ccp 1085. Motion for Writs of Administrative Mandamus and Ordinary Mandamus. is DENIED. The hearing decision directly contradicts HUD regulations. Further, Petitioner does not show that SFHA has a ministerial duty to follow the decisions of its hearing officers. The related declaratory relief requested is therefore denied. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LUMINOUS ION LLC VS. VICKI CHIAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Vicki Chiao And Mvc Real Estate, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 12, 2022, line 4. Defendants Vicki Chiao And Mvc Real Estate, Llc'S Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant's Motion to Compel Arbitration is CONTINUED to April 27, 2022. It has come to the Court's attention that the Court may have sustained Defendant's evidence filed in support of their Motion for Reconsideration in error, in part or in whole. The purpose of the continuance is for Defendant to re-file any evidence it wishes the Court to consider in determining the Motion to Compel the construction defect claims. Plaintiff may file an opposition to Defendant's evidence. Defendant shall file any evidence it wishes the Court to consider no later than April 15, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Plaintiff may file any objections to Defendant's evidence no later than April 20, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. No further briefing on the issues. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
